part.text:
# Manage Bookmarks in Jujutsu

## Background
In Jujutsu (`jj`), bookmarks are named pointers to revisions (similar to Git branches). They are used to mark specific commits, especially when integrating with Git remotes. This task tests your ability to create a bookmark and then move it to a different revision.

## Requirements
- You have a Jujutsu repository initialized at `/home/user/my-project`.
- Create a new bookmark named `feature-x` pointing to the initial working copy commit.
- Create a new commit on top of it with the description `add feature y`.
- Move the `feature-x` bookmark to point to this new commit.

## Implementation
1. Navigate to `/home/user/my-project`.
2. Create the bookmark `feature-x` on the current working copy.
3. Run `jj new -m "add feature y"` to create a new commit.
4. Move the `feature-x` bookmark to the new working copy commit.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/my-project`
- Use only `jj` commands.

# Manage Bookmarks in Jujutsu

## Background
In Jujutsu (`jj`), bookmarks are named pointers to revisions (similar to Git branches). They are used to mark specific commits, especially when integrating with Git remotes. This task tests your ability to create a bookmark and then move it to a different revision.

## Requirements
- You have a Jujutsu repository initialized at `/home/user/my-project`.
- Create a new bookmark named `feature-x` pointing to the initial working copy commit.
- Create a new commit on top of it with the description `add feature y`.
- Move the `feature-x` bookmark to point to this new commit.

## Implementation
1. Navigate to `/home/user/my-project`.
2. Create the bookmark `feature-x` on the current working copy.
3. Run `jj new -m "add feature y"` to create a new commit.
4. Move the `feature-x` bookmark to the new working copy commit.

## Constraints
- Project path: `/home/user/my-project`
- Use only `jj` commands.

output.output:
@  yuwmpnqm test@example.com 2026-04-07 22:10:37 a168e5f3
│  (empty) (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000

Warning: Target revision is empty.
Created 1 bookmarks pointing to yuwmpnqm a168e5f3 feature-x | (empty) (no description set)

input.command: cd /home/user/my-project && jj new -m "add feature y" && jj log --limit
cd /home/user/my-project && jj new -m "add feature y" && jj log --limit 5
cd /home/user/my-project && jj new -m "add feature y" && jj log --limit 5
cd /home/user/my-project && jj new -m "add feature y" && jj log --limit 5

output.output:
@  xonknkqy test@example.com 2026-04-22 06:44:20 1fc92949
│  (empty) add feature y
○  yuwmpnqm test@example.com 2026-04-07 22:10:37 feature-x a168e5f3
│  (empty) (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
Working copy  (@) now at: xonknkqy 1fc92949 (empty) add feature y
Parent commit (@-)      : yuwmpnqm a168e5f3 feature-x | (empty) (no description set)

input.command: cd /home/user/my-project && jj bookmark set feature-x -r @ && jj log --limit
cd /home/user/my-project && jj bookmark set feature-x -r @ && jj log --limit 5
cd /home/user/my-project && jj bookmark set feature-x -r @ && jj log --limit 5
cd /home/user/my-project && jj bookmark set feature-x -r @ && jj log --limit 5

output.output:
@  xonknkqy test@example.com 2026-04-22 06:44:20 feature-x 1fc92949
│  (empty) add feature y
○  yuwmpnqm test@example.com 2026-04-07 22:10:37 a168e5f3
│  (empty) (no description set)
◆  zzzzzzzz root() 00000000
Moved 1 bookmarks to xonknkqy 1fc92949 feature-x* | (empty) add feature y
